scope: Primary-source review of every positive pre-2008 hypothesis/mechanism pair. No hard-negative assertion and no ground-truth mutation.
digestPolicy: Generated dossier contentSha256 is SHA-256 of UTF-8 locator, newline and curated summary. It binds the local evidence assertion, not a remote byte snapshot.

evidenceAssertions.summary: The FDIC archive records Continental Illinois assistance in 1984 and the systemic implications considered by the deposit insurer.

evidenceAssertions.provider: Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System
evidenceAssertions.summary: The Federal Reserve study documents a broad run by U.S. and foreign institutional creditors and links liability concentration to systemic importance.

evidenceAssertions.provider: Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System
evidenceAssertions.summary: The guarantee slowed but did not immediately stop withdrawals, so the proposed July end boundary remains provisional rather than a proven recovery date.

evidenceAssertions.provider: Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System
evidenceAssertions.summary: The Federal Reserve study classifies the October 1987 crash as a major systemic shock in which asset prices fell and market functioning was severely impaired.
The CFTC chronology identifies October 19 as the largest one-day stock-price plunge then recorded and documents the agency's successive market-activity reports.
The CFTC reports that no CFTC-regulated system failed and no firm defaulted, limiting any claim that the crash produced universal infrastructure failure.
The BIS records severe Latin American turbulence during 1994, heavy Mexican outflows and regional reverberations after higher U.S. rates and the peso crisis.

evidenceAssertions.provider: Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System
evidenceAssertions.summary: FOMC minutes document sharp peso depreciation, spillovers to other developing economies and pressure on the dollar and U.S. policy expectations.

evidenceAssertions.provider: Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System
evidenceAssertions.summary: The same minutes describe robust domestic credit demands and monetary growth, weakening a claim of sustained broad U.S. market impairment across the full hypothesis window.
The BIS annual report documents the Asian crisis, volatile external funding reversals, dramatic market volatility and liquidity drying up in affected markets.

evidenceAssertions.provider: Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System
evidenceAssertions.summary: The Federal Reserve report records steep Asian currency and equity declines, an appreciating U.S. dollar and lower intermediate and long U.S. Treasury yields.

evidenceAssertions.provider: Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System
evidenceAssertions.summary: Despite foreign weakness, the report describes robust U.S. domestic demand, ample credit and rising equities in early 1998, limiting claims of broad domestic impairment.

evidenceAssertions.provider: Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System
evidenceAssertions.summary: The Federal Reserve report states that Russia's crisis triggered unusual volatility, wider risk spreads, higher uncertainty, lower equities and reduced market liquidity.
The BIS reports that Russia and LTCM sharply tightened issuance, curtailed bank and securities financing and reduced secondary trading in risky instruments.

evidenceAssertions.provider: Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System
evidenceAssertions.summary: The report also notes that most firms and households retained sufficient credit and spending was not materially constrained, narrowing the episode's systemic breadth.

dossierBlueprints.boundaryRationale: May begins with the documented run and intervention; July is retained from the hypothesis but recovery timing remains challenged by continuing withdrawals.
The monthly boundary isolates the October market break documented independently by the Federal Reserve and CFTC without relying on unavailable pre-1990 VIX history.
The narrower window begins with the peso break rather than the February U.S. rate move because available evidence does not yet prove broad impairment throughout 1994.
The window follows the peso crisis and documented regional and U.S. spillovers, while excluding earlier months supported only as general bond turbulence.
July starts with the Thai-baht break and January retains the acute regional transmission window before evidence becomes mixed with Russia and later global stress.
August begins with the Russian moratorium and October includes the documented global repricing and liquidity disruption before conditions eased later in the autumn.
The interval captures the Russian default, LTCM exposure shock and documented contraction in risky issuance, bank financing and secondary-market liquidity.
The interval covers transmission from the Russian moratorium through LTCM and the associated global pullback in risk-taking and international financing.

limitations: Evidence digests bind curated assertions and locators, not archived remote bytes.
All dossiers have one research reviewer and cannot be accepted until a second independent review.
No hard negative met the affirmative evidence contract in this increment.
The frozen E14.3 catalog associated VIX with 1987 despite VIX coverage beginning in 1990; the 1987 dossier replaces it with CFTC evidence without mutating the catalog.
